prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `03560426`.
codex_response:
Implemented `03560426` end-to-end. The rule is: take the non-background solid rectangles in left-to-right order, then rebuild them at the top-left so each next rectangle starts on the previous rectangle’s bottom-right cell, overwriting that shared corner.

I used the Opus hint as a starting point and discarded the Sonnet hint because it incorrectly describes a rotated bounding box; that mismatch is documented in [notes.md](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_03560426/notes.md). No new primitive was needed. The generator stays on-family by producing 10x10 inputs with 3-4 distinct bottom-aligned solid rectangles, one-column gaps, and size ranges constrained so the diagonal overlap chain fits the output.

`conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.verify --task 03560426` passed `4/4` official examples. `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.build --task 03560426 --n-examples 1000` succeeded, produced 1000 verified examples, and the generated preview sheet looked visually consistent with the originals.
